release_blockers: peer_identity_verified=false for current manual/local HTTP route
bridge HTTPS fingerprint pinning is not release-grade QUIC/TLS route proof
route evidence is not release-grade until the runtime route attempt records quic_tls_1_3
rendezvous target-candidate-assisted routing still needs real second-PC evidence
relay/tunnel fallback transport is not wired
route_explain.relay_policy: relay is Connect/Pro fallback only; it must not become the default data path

conclusion.release_interpretation: The local MUSU Desktop runtime is healthy. HUGH-MAIN is registered as a manual LAN peer but is unhealthy and not reachable on 192.168.1.192:8949. The selected route is HTTP bearer LAN with no verified peer identity, so even a successful connection would not be release-grade P2P proof until quic_tls_1_3 identity/encryption evidence is recorded.

next_required_actions: Install or start the current MUSU Desktop build on HUGH-MAIN.
Confirm HUGH-MAIN reports itself healthy through its local packaged MUSU status.
Confirm the reachable address and port for HUGH-MAIN and allow the port through the local firewall.
Rerun TCP reachability from HUGH_SECOND to the selected HUGH-MAIN endpoint.
Capture successful two-machine route evidence and runtime CPU matrix.
Then capture MUSU.PRO owner-scoped rendezvous, route metadata, relay transport proof, and payload delivery proof for hosted P2P release gates.
